title: Peloton Interactive (PTON) Q3 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Peloton Interactive Q3 2024 Earnings Call Summary. Leadership Changes: Barry McCarthy stepped down as President and CEO, with Karen Boone and Chris Bruzzo assuming interim co-CEO roles while the board searches for a permanent leader. Jay Hoag became board chairperson. Key Financial Metrics (Q3 FY2024): Total Revenue $718 million, Gross Margin 43.1%, Adjusted EBITDA $6 million, Free Cash Flow $9 million (first positive quarter in 13), Cash on Hand $795 million. Subscription Performance: Connected fitness subscriptions 3.06 million (+52k net adds), Monthly churn rate 1.2%, App subscriptions 674k (-44k sequentially), Subscription segment revenue $438 million at 68.1% margin. Restructuring Plan: Peloton announced $200 million in run-rate savings by end of fiscal 2025, involving approximately 400 employee departures (15% workforce reduction) and retail footprint optimization. Strategic Outlook: Management expressed confidence in achieving positive free cash flow in Q4 and maintaining it through fiscal 2025, though growth remains challenged by post-pandemic normalization in the connected fitness market.
